message.content:
Heydon Pickering -- Accessibility Review for Step 6: Projects (US-GTD-4) This is a substantial step. Lots to review. Here's my full assessment. ## What's good (no changes needed) - **Accessible names on all interactive elements**: `aria-label="Complete: {{ project.title }}"`, `aria-label="Delete: {{ project.title }}"`, `aria-label="Edit: {{ project.title }}"` on the project list. `aria-label="Complete: {{ action.title }}"` on next action items in the project detail. All include the item title. Correct. - **Heading hierarchy**: Project list has `

Projects

`. Project detail has `

{{ project.title }}

`, `

Next Actions

`, `

Completed Actions

`. No skipped levels. Correct. - **Section landmarks**: Project detail uses `
` and `
`. Screen reader users can navigate by landmark. Correct. - **Visible labels**: "Add project" label on title input, "Add next action" label on action input, "Context" visible label on context select. All properly associated via `for`/`id`. Learned from Steps 4-5. - **Focus management on projects list**: `project-focus.js` follows the established pattern correctly -- beforeSwap capture, afterSettle focus, edit mode transitions, empty state toggling. - **Stalled project indicator uses text, not color alone**: "No next actions" text label on stalled projects in the list, plus descriptive notice on the detail page. Meets WCAG 1.4.1. This is exactly what I asked for in my Step 6 preview. - **Completed actions visual treatment**: Strikethrough + muted color (two indicators). Checked icon `aria-hidden="true"` correctly hides the decorative character from screen readers. - **Forced-colors support**: Complete button on project actions has `border: 1px solid ButtonText` in forced-colors mode. - **Error display**: Uses `role="alert"` for errors, `role="status"` for the stalled notice. Appropriate distinction. - **Progressive enhancement**: All forms work without JS. Project title links are real `` elements. Good. - **Breadcrumb navigation**: Project detail has a "Projects" link back to the list. Simple and effective. - **Live region announcements**: "Project added", "Project updated", "Project completed", "Project deleted" all fire via `htmx_response_with_announce`. - **Page title**: `{{ project.title }} -- Projects -- Todo List` includes the project name. Correct. ## BLOCKING Issues **1. Focus management missing on project detail page** The project detail page (`project_detail.html`) has no focus management script. When a next action is completed via HTMX on the project detail page, the `
  • ` is removed (same `hx-swap="outerHTML"` with empty body pattern). Focus falls to ``. The project detail page needs focus management for: - Completing an action (removes `
  • ` from active list) - The fallback should be the next/previous sibling `.project-action-item`, then `#project-action-title` (the add input) This could be handled by either: - A separate `project-detail-focus.js`, or - Extending `project-focus.js` to also handle `.project-action-item` elements **2. Stalled flag text color fails contrast on its background** The `.project-item__stalled-flag` uses `color: var(--color-warning)` which is `oklch(0.75 0.15 75)` on `background-color: var(--color-warning-light)` which is `oklch(0.93 0.04 75)`. Text at lightness 0.75 on background at lightness 0.93 gives approximately 1.5-2:1 contrast ratio. WCAG AA requires 4.5:1 for normal text. **Fix**: Use `color: var(--color-fg)` (which is `--color-gray-800`, lightness 0.27) or a darker warning color for the text. The warning-light background is fine -- just the text needs more contrast. The detail page stalled notice already does this correctly (`color: var(--color-fg)`), so the fix is just aligning the list flag to match. ## Minor Issues **3. Project detail empty state for active actions is not always in DOM** Line 59-61 of `project_detail.html`: ```html {% if active_actions.is_empty() %}

    No active next actions.

    {% endif %} ``` This uses conditional rendering (`{% if %}`) instead of the always-in-DOM pattern with `hidden` that we adopted in Step 5 for the inbox. When the last action is completed, the empty state text cannot be shown by JS because it doesn't exist in the DOM. This is Minor because the live region announcement ("Action completed") still communicates what happened. But for visual consistency, the always-in-DOM pattern would be better. **4. Edit form Save/Cancel buttons lack unique accessible names (same as Step 4)** The project edit form has `` and `    Cancel` without unique accessible names. Same deferred item from Step 4. Noting for consistency. ## Nice-to-have **5. The project title link in the list could benefit from a more descriptive accessible name** `{{ project.title }}` -- the accessible name is just the project title. Adding "View project: {{ project.title }}" as an aria-label would make it clearer when navigating by links. Not blocking because the context (being in a list item with other controls) makes it reasonably clear. --- **Summary**: Two BLOCKING items (focus management on project detail page, stalled flag contrast). Two minor (empty state pattern, Save/Cancel names). One nice-to-have. The core accessible names, heading hierarchy, section landmarks, visible labels, and progressive enhancement are all correct from the initial implementation. Good improvement from the earlier steps.

content.text: Stale Step 6 review. Step 6 has 9/9 consensus and all blocking items were resolved. Idle, waiting for Carson's Step 7 code.
